summary: Cuba's National Electric System collapsed on March 16, 2026. BGP collector RIB visibility remained at 4/4 across all sampled collectors — the routing control plane did not see the disruption. This demonstrates that BGP visibility is one layer of the Internet, not the whole Internet. Power, access, CPE, and data-plane failures can cause massive user disruption while BGP routes remain stable.

externalEventContext.title: Cuba national power-grid collapse — March 16, 2026
externalEventContext.source: Cloudflare Radar / AP / Internet Society Pulse
externalEventContext.note: Cuba's National Electric System collapsed on March 16, 2026, causing a total disconnection (~65% traffic drop per Cloudflare Radar, 30+ hours). ReachMap shows BGP collector RIB visibility during the collapse — did Cuban prefixes disappear from the global routing table? Result: BGP visibility remained at 4/4. The routing control plane did not see the disruption because the failure was at the physical power, access, and data-plane layers.
